components.name: ammonium sulfate ((NH4)2SO4)
components.evidence: 5x M63 salts per liter: 10 g (NH4)2SO4, 68 g KH2PO4, 2.5 mg FeSO4.7H2O, adjust to pH 7 with KOH. Paper uses 20% of 5x = 1x, giving 2 g/L (NH4)2SO4.

components.name: monopotassium phosphate (KH2PO4)
components.evidence: To prepare 1 liter of 5X M63 stock, add: 10 g (NH4)2SO4, 68 g KH2PO4, and 2.5 mg FeSO4.7H2O. At 1x (20% of 5x) this is 13.6 g/L KH2PO4.

components.name: ferrous sulfate heptahydrate (FeSO4.7H2O)
components.evidence: 5X M63 salts contain 2.5 mg FeSO4.7H2O per liter; at 1x (20% of 5x) = 0.5 mg/L. This is the only trace metal in M63 (M63 has no separate trace-element or vitamin mix beyond thiamine).

components.name: potassium hydroxide (KOH)
components.evidence: Adjust to pH 7 with KOH. Converts part of the monobasic KH2PO4 buffer to dibasic and raises total K+.

components.name: magnesium sulfate (MgSO4)
components.evidence: M63 minimal media consisting of 20% 5 x M63 salt [70], 1 ug/ml B1, 2 x 10-3 M MgSO4, and supplemented with 20 ug/ml glucose (M63+glu).

components.name: thiamine (vitamin B1)
components.evidence: ...20% 5 x M63 salt [70], 1 ug/ml B1, 2 x 10-3 M MgSO4... (B1 = thiamine; ~3 uM, salt form unspecified so mM left null).

components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence: ...and supplemented with 20 ug/ml glucose (M63+glu). (Main results text writes '20 um/ml' which is a typo for 20 ug/ml as stated in Methods.)

components.name: ampicillin
components.role: selection antibiotic (plasmid maintenance; not a nutrient)
components.evidence: Both media were supplemented with 100 ug/ml ampicillin, unless otherwise stated.

components.name: water
components.evidence: Add the reagents to a 2-liter flask with 1 liter of high quality distilled water.

components.name: O2 (dissolved oxygen)
components.evidence: Using these small volumes, the matrix thickness was less than 400 um, which ensures a minimum of oxygen depletion. Cultures are aerobic (E. coli grown in thin agarose with ambient O2).

media.base_medium: M63 minimal medium (Elbing & Brent 2002, Current Protocols in Molecular Biology, 1.1.1-1.1.7)

sources_chased.what: base recipe cited as ref 70 (Elbing & Brent 2002, 5x M63 salts)
sources_chased.how: WebSearch of Elbing & Brent 2002 Current Protocols M63 recipe (Wiley DOI 10.1002/0471142727.mb0101s59 returned 403; recipe confirmed via multiple protocol mirrors: protocolsonline, laboratorynotes, PMC6819147)

missing_before: identity/concentration of base salts: (NH4)2SO4 (2 g/L), KH2PO4 (13.6 g/L)
FeSO4.7H2O 0.5 mg/L (iron was present as EX_fe2_e but no source/conc)
media.notes: M63+glu is a fully defined minimal medium; the paper gives only supplements (B1, MgSO4, glucose) and cites ref 70 for the 5x M63 salt base, which supplies ammonium, phosphate/K buffer, and the sole trace metal (FeSO4). M63 by definition contains no additional trace-element mix and thiamine is its only vitamin, so the recipe is complete. The GEM exchange list already covered every ion; this pass recovers compound identities, exact concentrations, KOH and ampicillin.
